examples.sentence: At the festival, the kids decided to go moggy and climbed all over the stage, ignoring the usual rules.
examples.translation: At the festival, the kids decided to behave wildly and climbed all over the stage, ignoring the usual rules.

examples.sentence: If you let him go moggy like that, there's no telling what adventures he'll get into during the camping trip.
examples.translation: If you let him act without restraint like that, there's no telling what adventures he'll get into during the camping trip.

examples.sentence: Last weekend, the group went moggy at the concert, dancing and cheering like there was no tomorrow.
examples.translation: Last weekend, the group behaved wildly at the concert, dancing and cheering like there was no tomorrow.

examples.sentence: After days without sleep, she started to go moggy, talking to shadows and laughing uncontrollably.
examples.translation: After days without sleep, she started to become mentally disturbed, talking to shadows and laughing uncontrollably.

examples.sentence: Do you think he’s really gone moggy from the stress, or is he just joking around?
examples.translation: Do you think he’s really become delirious from the stress, or is he just joking around?

examples.sentence: The patient had gone moggy during the fever, exhibiting confused and erratic behavior throughout the night.
examples.translation: The patient had become delirious during the fever, exhibiting confused and erratic behavior throughout the night.

examples.sentence: The old engine had gone moggy after years of neglect and wouldn’t start anymore.
examples.translation: The old engine had become broken and dysfunctional after years of neglect and wouldn’t start anymore.

examples.sentence: We found that the software had gone moggy overnight, crashing every time we tried to run it.
examples.translation: We found that the software had become disorganized and dysfunctional overnight, crashing every time we tried to run it.

examples.sentence: By the time I checked the equipment, it had already gone moggy and was useless for the project.
examples.translation: By the time I checked the equipment, it had already become broken and useless for the project.

examples.sentence: During the summer holidays, the children would go moggy in the backyard, exploring every corner with wild abandon.
examples.translation: During the summer holidays, the children would behave without restraint in the backyard, exploring every corner with wild abandon.

examples.sentence: When left alone at the park, the puppies tend to go moggy, chasing their tails and tumbling over each other.
examples.translation: When left alone at the park, the puppies tend to behave wildly, chasing their tails and tumbling over each other.

examples.sentence: The teenagers decided to go moggy at the amusement park, racing from ride to ride without stopping.
examples.translation: The teenagers decided to act adventurously at the amusement park, racing from ride to ride without stopping.

examples.sentence: If you let your imagination go moggy during writing exercises, you might come up with some very creative stories.
examples.translation: If you let your imagination go wild during writing exercises, you might come up with some very creative stories.

examples.sentence: After living in Bali for two years, Sarah really began to go native, wearing traditional clothes and enjoying the local cuisine daily.
examples.translation: After living in Bali for two years, Sarah really began to adopt the lifestyle and customs of the locals, wearing traditional clothes and enjoying the local cuisine daily.

examples.sentence: He went native so quickly during his posting in Morocco that his colleagues barely recognized him anymore.
examples.translation: He adopted the lifestyle and customs of the local inhabitants so quickly during his posting in Morocco that his colleagues barely recognized him anymore.

examples.sentence: If you go native while working abroad, it can be a wonderful way to immerse yourself in the culture and make new friends.
examples.translation: If you adopt the lifestyle and customs of the locals while working abroad, it can be a wonderful way to immerse yourself in the culture and make new friends.

examples.sentence: The diplomat was accused of going native after he started siding with the local protesters against his own government.
examples.translation: The diplomat was accused of developing such sympathy with the local population that he compromised his original duties by siding with the protesters.

examples.sentence: You have to be careful not to go native when working as a journalist abroad, or you might lose your objectivity.
examples.translation: You have to be careful not to develop too much sympathy with the locals when working as a journalist abroad, or you might lose your objectivity.

examples.sentence: Some colonists went native, adopting local customs so fully that they abandoned their original loyalties entirely.
examples.translation: Some colonists developed such identification with the local population that they abandoned their original loyalties entirely.

examples.sentence: After six months working through the agency, John decided to go native and accepted a direct position with the client company.
examples.translation: After six months working through the agency, John decided to cease agency work and began working directly as an employee for the client company.

examples.sentence: Do you think she’ll go native soon, or will she keep consulting through the agency?
examples.translation: Do you think she will stop working through the agency soon, or will she keep consulting through it?

examples.sentence: The consultant has gone native and is now a full-time employee at the firm, no longer contracted through the agency.
examples.translation: The consultant has ceased agency work and is now a full-time employee at the firm.

examples.sentence: When Mark went native during his stay in the Amazon, he adopted the local dress and learned their language fluently.
examples.translation: When Mark adopted the lifestyle and customs of the local inhabitants during his stay in the Amazon, he wore their dress and learned their language fluently.

examples.sentence: Travelers who go native often find deeper meaning in their journeys by fully embracing local traditions and habits.
examples.translation: Travelers who adopt the lifestyle and appearance of locals often find deeper meaning in their journeys.

examples.sentence: The explorer’s journals showed how he went native among the tribes, sharing their daily routines and ceremonies.
examples.translation: The explorer’s journals showed how he adopted the customs and lifestyle of the tribes, sharing their daily routines and ceremonies.

examples.sentence: It wasn’t long before the expatriates went native, blending seamlessly into the village community.
examples.translation: It wasn’t long before the expatriates adopted the lifestyle and customs of the locals, blending seamlessly into the village community.

examples.sentence: Everyone knew that old car was a gone case; no amount of repairs could bring it back to life.
examples.translation: Everyone knew that old car was hopeless and beyond help; no amount of repairs could restore it.

examples.sentence: You can’t save him from his bad habits anymore—he’s a gone case at this point.
examples.translation: You can’t help him anymore; he’s hopeless due to his bad habits.

examples.sentence: After the accident, the computer was a gone case, completely fried beyond any chance of recovery.
examples.translation: After the accident, the computer was irredeemably damaged and beyond recovery.

examples.sentence: That old watch is a gone case; it stopped ticking years ago and nobody can fix it now.
examples.translation: That old watch is irredeemable; it stopped working years ago and nobody can repair it now.

examples.sentence: When the project failed so spectacularly, the manager declared it a gone case and moved on.
examples.translation: When the project failed so badly, the manager regarded it as hopeless and moved on.

examples.sentence: The abandoned house had become a gone case, with broken windows and collapsing walls everywhere.
examples.translation: The abandoned house was hopeless and beyond repair, with broken windows and collapsing walls.

examples.sentence: We found the laptop was a gone case after the flood damaged its internal parts beyond repair.
examples.translation: We found the laptop irreparably damaged and beyond help after the flood.

examples.sentence: On Sunday mornings, dad has usually gone fishing at the lake near our cabin.
examples.translation: On Sunday mornings, dad is usually engaged in the activity of fishing at the lake near our cabin.

examples.sentence: They’ve gone fishing every weekend this summer, hoping to catch something big for dinner.
examples.translation: They have been engaged in fishing every weekend this summer, hoping to catch something big for dinner.

examples.sentence: Have you gone fishing lately, or have you been too busy with work?
examples.translation: Have you been engaged in fishing recently, or have you been too busy with work?

examples.sentence: We will go fishing tomorrow if the weather stays nice and the river isn’t too high.
examples.translation: We will engage in fishing tomorrow if the weather remains good and the river is not too high.

examples.sentence: I tried calling Mike at the office, but he’s gone fishing this week and won’t respond until Monday.
examples.translation: I tried calling Mike at the office, but he is unavailable because he has taken time off for leisure this week.

examples.sentence: When the CEO went fishing, the whole department slowed down until she returned.
examples.translation: When the CEO took time off for leisure, the whole department slowed down until her return.

examples.sentence: Don’t expect a quick reply from him; he’s gone fishing and ignoring all emails for a while.
examples.translation: Don’t expect a quick reply from him; he is unavailable because he is taking time off for recreation.

examples.sentence: During the meeting, Jenny seemed gone fishing, staring out the window and missing almost everything discussed.
examples.translation: During the meeting, Jenny was mentally absent and inattentive, staring out the window and missing almost everything.

examples.sentence: Are you gone fishing again? You didn’t answer any of my questions just now.
examples.translation: Are you mentally absent again? You didn’t answer any of my questions just now.

examples.sentence: He’s gone fishing lately, unable to focus on his work or conversations properly.
examples.translation: He has been mentally distracted lately, unable to focus on his work or conversations.

examples.sentence: After the accident, everyone thought he’d gone fishing, as his behavior became erratic and strange.
examples.translation: After the accident, everyone thought he had become crazy or mentally unwell, as his behavior became erratic and strange.

examples.sentence: She’s definitely gone fishing after that traumatic event; her family is worried about her mental health.
examples.translation: She has become mentally unwell after that traumatic event; her family is worried about her mental health.

examples.sentence: If you don’t get enough rest, you might go fishing and start acting irrationally.
examples.translation: If you don’t get enough rest, you might become mentally disturbed and start acting irrationally.

examples.sentence: The captain’s log noted that the sailor had gone north about during the storm, not by drowning but from illness.
examples.translation: The captain’s log noted that the sailor had died during the storm by a cause other than drowning.

examples.sentence: Many sailors in the fleet had gone north about after the harsh winter, their deaths recorded carefully in the ship’s records.
examples.translation: Many sailors had died from causes other than drowning during the harsh winter, recorded carefully in the ship’s records.

examples.sentence: It was a sad day when old Jim gone north about, succumbing to disease far from the sea.
examples.translation: It was a sad day when old Jim died, succumbing to disease rather than drowning.

examples.sentence: The sailor had gone north about during the voyage, his body never recovered from the shipwreck.
examples.translation: The sailor was dead as a result of causes other than drowning during the voyage.

examples.sentence: Several crew members went north about in the harsh conditions, and their families were notified promptly.
examples.translation: Several crew members died due to causes other than drowning in the harsh conditions, and their families were notified.

examples.sentence: Reports confirm that the victim gone north about was due to pneumonia, not drowning as originally feared.
examples.translation: Reports confirm that the person’s death was due to pneumonia, not drowning.

examples.sentence: It was recorded officially that the sailor had gone north about after falling ill on board the ship.
examples.translation: It was officially recorded that the sailor had died after falling ill on board the ship.

examples.sentence: The papers flew off the desk and were gone with the wind before anyone could catch them.
examples.translation: The papers were carried away and dispersed by the wind before anyone could catch them.

examples.sentence: Her hopes of a promotion were gone with the wind after the sudden company reorganization.
examples.translation: Her hopes of a promotion were swept away or removed due to the company reorganization.

examples.sentence: As soon as the storm hit, the picnic decorations were gone with the wind, scattered across the park.
examples.translation: As soon as the storm hit, the picnic decorations were physically carried away and dispersed by the wind.

examples.sentence: She was heartbroken when her beloved pet passed away but comforted herself knowing he was gone with the wind.
examples.translation: She was heartbroken when her pet died, using an expression meaning deceased.

examples.sentence: When Grandpa finally went with the wind, the whole family gathered to celebrate his life.
examples.translation: When Grandpa passed away, the whole family gathered to celebrate his life.

examples.sentence: The soldier was said to have gone with the wind during the war, his fate mourned by many.
examples.translation: The soldier was said to have died during the war, his death mourned by many.

examples.sentence: All their savings were gone with the wind after the market crash wiped out their investments.
examples.translation: All their savings were completely lost or destroyed after the market crash.

examples.sentence: The old traditions have sadly gone with the wind in this fast-changing world.
examples.translation: The old traditions have completely vanished and are no longer existent.

examples.sentence: When the fire spread through the village, many homes were gone with the wind, leaving nothing behind.
examples.translation: When the fire spread, many homes were completely destroyed and vanished.

examples.sentence: After the storm, the leaves and branches were gone with the wind, scattered across the streets.
examples.translation: After the storm, the leaves and branches were physically carried away and dispersed by the wind.

examples.sentence: The kite slipped from her grasp and was gone with the wind, disappearing into the clouds.
examples.translation: The kite was carried away by the wind and vanished from sight.

examples.sentence: The papers on his desk went with the wind as the window blew open unexpectedly.
examples.translation: The papers on his desk were dispersed by the wind when the window blew open.

examples.sentence: The scent of the flowers was gone with the wind soon after the breeze started blowing through the garden.
examples.translation: The scent of the flowers was carried away and dispersed by the wind.

examples.sentence: All our talks about changing the policy seem to go nowhere; nothing ever gets decided.
examples.translation: All our discussions about changing the policy fail to make progress and lead to no useful outcome.

examples.sentence: If this project keeps going nowhere, we might have to rethink our whole approach.
examples.translation: If this project continues to fail to make progress, we might need to rethink our approach.

examples.sentence: Why do you think the negotiations have gone nowhere despite weeks of talks?
examples.translation: Why do you think the negotiations have failed to progress despite weeks of talks?

examples.sentence: Due to the lockdown, many people have to go nowhere and stay inside their homes all day.
examples.translation: Due to the lockdown, many people are physically unable to go anywhere and must remain inside.

examples.sentence: She was told to go nowhere until the doctor gave her permission to leave the hospital.
examples.translation: She was instructed not to go anywhere physically until cleared by the doctor.

examples.sentence: When the storm hit, the hikers had to go nowhere, waiting out the weather in their tents.
examples.translation: When the storm came, the hikers were unable to go anywhere and waited in their tents.

examples.sentence: The child refused to go nowhere, insisting on staying in the same spot despite the chaos around him.
examples.translation: The child refused to move anywhere, insisting on staying put despite chaos.

examples.sentence: They’re working hard on the project, but it seems to go nowhere fast with all the delays and setbacks.
examples.translation: They are making rapid efforts but failing to make progress on the project due to delays.

examples.sentence: With so many meetings and no decisions, this discussion is going nowhere fast.
examples.translation: With many meetings and no decisions, the discussion is rapidly failing to achieve progress.

examples.sentence: If your career feels like it’s going nowhere fast, maybe it’s time to consider a change.
examples.translation: If your career is making no progress despite much activity, it might be time for a change.

examples.sentence: Despite the car’s engine revving loudly, it was going nowhere fast because the brakes were on.
examples.translation: Despite the car’s engine running rapidly, it made no physical progress because the brakes were applied.

examples.sentence: The hamster wheel spins quickly but goes nowhere fast, just like the animal running in place.
examples.translation: The hamster wheel rotates rapidly but does not move forward physically.

examples.sentence: She was running on the treadmill but going nowhere fast since the machine was stuck in one spot.
examples.translation: She was running rapidly on the treadmill without physical displacement.

examples.sentence: The boat engine revved, but caught in the mud, it went nowhere fast despite the noise.
examples.translation: The boat’s engine made noise but the boat made no progress because it was stuck.

examples.sentence: He was a good and honest man, respected by all who knew him.
examples.translation: He was a man who was both good and honest, respected by everyone.

examples.sentence: The recipe calls for fresh tomatoes and good onions to bring out the flavor.
examples.translation: The recipe requires fresh tomatoes and good onions to enhance the taste.

examples.sentence: She is both good and kind, always helping those in need without hesitation.
examples.translation: She has qualities of being good and kind, always willing to help.

examples.sentence: They offered a good and affordable service, making them popular among customers.
examples.translation: They provided a service that was both good and affordable, popular with customers.

examples.sentence: Make sure you’re good and ready before we leave for the trip early tomorrow morning.
examples.translation: Make sure you are completely ready before we depart early tomorrow.

examples.sentence: I was good and tired after the long hike, but the view made it worth it.
examples.translation: I was thoroughly tired after the long hike, but the view was worth it.

examples.sentence: They were good and surprised by the unexpected party their friends threw for them.
examples.translation: They were completely surprised by the unexpected party.

examples.sentence: After helping the boss with that difficult project, I think I’m finally in her good books.
examples.translation: After assisting the boss, I believe I have gained her favorable regard.

examples.sentence: You’ll stay in the teacher’s good books if you turn your homework in on time every week.
examples.translation: You will be regarded favorably by the teacher if you submit homework punctually.

examples.sentence: He’s been in the manager’s good books since he took on extra shifts without complaint.
examples.translation: He has earned the manager’s personal approval by working extra shifts willingly.

examples.sentence: I just finished reading some good books on medieval history that I highly recommend.
examples.translation: I completed reading several high-quality, enjoyable books on medieval history.

examples.sentence: She collects good books, always searching for rare editions in local bookstores.
examples.translation: She collects enjoyable and worthwhile books, often seeking rare editions.

examples.sentence: The library’s new section has many good books about science and technology.
examples.translation: The library’s new collection includes many quality books about science and technology.

examples.sentence: If you want to learn French, starting with good books makes all the difference.
examples.translation: Starting with high-quality and useful books makes learning French easier.

examples.sentence: The little boy was such a good boy, always listening carefully and helping his parents.
examples.translation: The young male child was well-behaved and obedient, always helping his parents.

examples.sentence: Our dog is a good boy; he never barks unnecessarily and is great with children.
examples.translation: Our male dog is obedient and well-behaved, never barking unnecessarily and good with kids.

examples.sentence: You’ve been a good boy today, so here’s a treat for you.
examples.translation: You have behaved well today, so here is a reward.

examples.sentence: That good boy in the class always finishes his homework on time and helps others.
examples.translation: That obedient male student always completes homework on time and assists classmates.

examples.sentence: Come on, good boy, just do what I’m asking and this will all go smoothly.
examples.translation: Come on, do what I’m asking in a patronizing way, implying compliance.

examples.sentence: He smiled and said, ‘Good boy,’ after his colleague agreed to cover his shift, though it sounded a bit condescending.
examples.translation: He said ‘Good boy’ ironically to his colleague, implying a patronizing approval.

examples.sentence: Don’t just sit there—be a good boy and help me finish these reports before the deadline.
examples.translation: In a demeaning or cajoling manner, telling someone to comply and help complete the work.
